root_text.sn32.56:1.2: Ekamantaṁ nisinno kho so bhikkhu bhagavantaṁ etadavoca:
root_text.sn32.56:1.3: “ko nu kho, bhante, hetu, ko paccayo, yenekadā vāto hotī”ti?
root_text.sn32.56:1.6: ‘yannūna mayaṁ sakāya ratiyā vaseyyāmā’ti, tesaṁ taṁ cetopaṇidhimanvāya vāto hoti.
root_text.sn32.56:1.7: Ayaṁ kho, bhikkhu, hetu, ayaṁ paccayo, yenekadā vāto hotī”ti.

translation_text.sn32.56:1.2: Seated to one side, that mendicant said to the Buddha:
translation_text.sn32.56:1.3: “Sir, what is the cause, what is the reason why sometimes it becomes windy?”
translation_text.sn32.56:1.4: “Mendicant, there are what are called gods of the windy thunderclouds.
translation_text.sn32.56:1.6: ‘Why don’t we revel in our own kind of enjoyment?’ Then, in accordance with their wish, it becomes windy.
translation_text.sn32.56:1.7: This is the cause, this is the reason why sometimes it becomes windy.”
